summary: ts-einkle-slot impersonates big.js and sideloads undeclared module via transitive dep
details: Package publishes under the name 'ts-einkle-slot' while copying big.js's description, author (M8ch88l@gmail.com), and repository (MikeMcl/big.js) verbatim to impersonate the legitimate big.js library. On require/import the main entry runs a try/catch that dynamically requires the undeclared module 'node-slot' and invokes doc.from_str(), with all errors silently swallowed. 'node-slot' is not listed in dependencies, so the resolved code comes from whatever sibling supplies that name in the installer's tree. The declared runtime dependency 'ts-einkle' (^1.1.3) — unrelated to big.js (which has zero runtime dependencies) and sharing the typosquat's naming prefix — is the staging vehicle that makes 'node-slot' resolvable at import time. The net effect: any consumer who installs this thinking it is big.js silently pulls an attacker-controlled transitive whose code executes on the first import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
